title: Use ARIA Only to Fill a Real Semantic Gap
summary: Build and verify aria use in custom survey behavior architecture review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain aria use from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply aria use without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the custom survey behavior architecture review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves aria use when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: custom survey behavior architecture review.
steps.why: Use ARIA Only to Fill a Real Semantic Gap makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in custom survey behavior architecture review.

content.text: ARIA changes exposed semantics; it does not add focus, keyboard behavior, form participation, validation, or state logic. Native HTML is the first choice when it provides the needed contract. When a genuine custom pattern remains, its role, states, properties, keyboard interactions, focus management, and state updates must stay synchronized. Apply it to custom survey behavior architecture review.
